data.title: FAA set to ease restrictions on Boeing aircraft deliveries - WSJ
data.description:
Boeing (NYSE:BA) +3.6% pre-market Friday after The Wall Street Journal reported the planemaker is expected to regain authority from the Federal Aviation Administration to perform final safety checks on its 737 MAX jets, a sign that U.S. government officials are becoming more satisfied with the company's effort to improve its manufacturing process.

The FAA often delegates routine inspections to aerospace companies to focus personnel on more critical tasks, but the agency tightened oversight of Boeing (NYSE:BA) in 2019 after two deadly 737 MAX crashes.

The restrictions have limited Boeing's (BA) flexibility in delivering planes to customers, having to work on government employees' workday schedules to get safety approvals, so returning some of the authority to the company could free up FAA inspectors to more closely monitor the production of planes, rather than paperwork at the end of the process, according to WSJ.

The FAA is also expected to allow Boeing (BA) to increase its production rate for the 737 MAX to 42 planes per month from a current cap of 38, according to the report.

Regulators, citing Boeing's (BA) emphasis on production over quality, imposed the production limit after the January 2024 midair blowout of fuselage panel in an Alaska Airlines jet.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: META, COST, REGN, XOM
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday, as the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge came inline. Here are four stocks to watch on the day: 

Meta Platforms (META) shares were largely unchanged in premarket trade as the firm said it will soon launch paid versions of Facebook and Instagram in the U.K., which will remove advertising from both social media platforms. Meta has set the subscription for no ads cost at £2.99/month on the web or £3.99/month on iOS and Android for the first Meta account.

Costco (COST) stock was down 0.7% in extended trading after its comparable sales in the U.S. were slightly below expectations and overall company comp sales decelerated from the previous quarter. The development was unexpected given the lingering inflationary and tariff environment that favors value positioning among consumers. However, the firm beat its top and bottom line estimates in the fourth quarter. 

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN) shares rose 0.9% in premarket trade as the firm said that the U.S. FDA approved a label expansion for its anti-cholesterol agent Evkeeza, marketed globally with Ultragenyx Pharmaceuticals. Accordingly, the injectable, first approved in 2021 for adults and adolescents with homozygous familial hypercholesterolemia ((HoFH)), will also be available for U.S. children aged 1–5 years with the rare disorder when added to diet and exercise and other lipid-lowering agents.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) stock hugged the flat line before the opening bell as the firm is said to have doubled the number of traders working in the U.K. over the past two years, as the oil and gas major aims to profit more from its global energy infrastructure. The energy giant now has about 300 traders, analysts, and support staff operating in London and continues to recruit to build out its network globally.

data.title: Kyndryl gets initiated with balanced views at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley began coverage of IT infrastructure and cloud services provider Kyndryl (NYSE:KD) with an Equal-weight rating and a $30 price target.

Analysts led by James Faucette said the year-to-date, or YTD, share decline reflects investor caution and mounting skepticism around Kyndryl's ability to achieve its fiscal year 2028 targets.

However, the analysts added that the +35% gain over the last 12 months suggests the market still expects some degree of improvement in growth and profitability, even if the company ultimately falls short of its long-term objectives.

"We similarly believe KD is making steady progress on its self-help initiatives and with shares down (14)% YTD, we think the stock already embeds meaningful uncertainty around achieving FY28 targets, which underscores our balanced view," said Faucette and his team.

data.title: Amneal seeks FDA nod for generic targeting Xolair marketed by Roche and Novartis
data.description:
• Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) on Friday announced the FDA submissions to seek U.S. approval for an off-patent version of Xolair, an asthma therapy marketed by Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) and Novartis (NYSE:NVS) globally.
  • Once approved, the treatment developed by Amneal’s (NASDAQ:AMRX) partner Kashiv BioSciences is set to target a blockbuster medication that generated roughly $4.1B in sales in the U.S. over 12 months ending July 2025, according to data from healthcare analytics firm IQVIA (IQV).
  • Xolair, also known as omalizumab, is approved in the U.S. for several inflammatory conditions, including asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps.
  • Amneal (NASDAQ:AMRX) said that the submission of its Biologics License Application indicated an accelerated timeline for its marketing efforts for the drug. As a result, the company will incur a $22.5M R&D milestone charge in Q3, as opposed to its prior plans to record the expense in Q4.

data.title: Intel, GlobalFoundries gain ground on Trump's potential 1-to-1 chip rule
data.description:
Shares of some U.S. semiconductor companies, including Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) and GlobalFoundries (NASDAQ:GFS), gained ground on Friday following reports the Trump Administration is planning a new policy that would require companies to manufacture the same number of chips in the U.S. as their customers import from overseas producers.

This rule would create a 1:1 domestic-to-imported chips ratio.

Intel was up 4.3% during pre-market trading and GlobalFoundries had jumped 7.6%. Intel, Micron Technology (MU), GlobalFoundries (NASDAQ:GFS), TSMC (TSM) and Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) are all building multibillion-dollar U.S. facilities. Micron edged up 1%.

Under the proposed system, if a company commits to producing one million chips in the U.S., its customers can continue importing that quantity without tariffs until the factory is operational.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: United Airlines lands key FAA certification for Starlink on mainline flights
data.description:
United Airlines Holdings (NASDAQ:UAL) announced on Friday that the FAA certified its first mainline Starlink-equipped aircraft. The certification comes less than five months since the first Starlink-equipped United (NASDAQ:UAL) customer flight on the Embraer 175 regional aircraft and less than a year since the airline company signed the industry's largest agreement of its kind with SpaceX (SPACE) to bring Starlink's (STRLK) Wi-Fi service free to MileagePlus members on the airline's mainline and regional aircraft fleet.

The company said the first mainline commercial flight enabled with Starlink (STRLK) is planned for October 15.

United's (UAL) first Starlink-equipped regional flight took place on a regional jet in May, and the airline now has Starlink (STRLK) installed on more than half of the aircraft in its regional fleet. United (UAL) said initial customer scores on those flights have been positive. Starlink (STRLK) is free for all United (UAL) MileagePlus customers.

Shares of UAL edged 0.7% higher in premarket trading on Friday to $99.83. The airline stock is up 26% over the last six months but still trades well below the 52-week high of $116.00.

data.title: SS Innovations appoints CFO
data.description:
SS Innovations International (NASDAQ:SSII) announced on Friday the appointment of Naveen Kumar Amar as CFO, effective September 24.

Shares were -1.59% pre-market to $5.56.

Amar succeeds Vishwa Srivastava, the interim CFO since July 2025. Srivastava will continue as the CEO - Asia Pacific.

Most recently, Amar provided virtual CFO services to clients based in the U.S., Canada, and the UK.
